articles.title: Dashlane Discloses Brute-Force Attack, Encrypted Vaults of Fewer Than 20 Users Downloaded
articles.summary: Password manager Dashlane has disclosed that "fewer than" 20 users on the personal subscription plan had their encrypted vaults downloaded following a brute-force attack launched by an unknown party. On May 31, 2026, the company said an "external" threat actor launched a brute-force attack against certain Dashlane user accounts with the aim of breaking two-factor authentication (2FA)

keypoints: Brute-force attack compromised fewer than 20 encrypted vaults
Two-factor authentication bypassed by unknown threat actor

analysis.title: Attack Scope and Impact
analysis.desc: Dashlane reports limited breach affecting personal subscription users only. Encrypted vaults downloaded but content protection status unclear.

analysis.title: Attack Method and Vulnerability
analysis.desc: External threat actor exploited brute-force technique targeting 2FA mechanisms. Suggests password weakness or authentication process vulnerability.

analysis.title: Response and User Recommendations
analysis.desc: Company disclosed incident May 31, 2026. Users should enable stronger 2FA, update master passwords, monitor account activity.

ai_summary.suggested_title: Dashlane Brute-Force Attack Breaches 20 User Vaults
